forecasts.reasoning:
Discord initiated IPO preparations by hiring bankers in March 2025, which typically precedes an S-1 filing by 3-6 months. In late April 2025, Jason Citron stepped down as CEO, handing the reins to Humam Sakhnini (formerly of Activision) specifically to guide Discord toward public markets. However, in his blog post, Citron referred only to "someday becoming a public company," a phrasing explicitly excluded by the resolution criteria as too vague. 

To resolve "Yes," Discord must either file an S-1 or make a second, highly specific announcement before July 1, 2025. Given there are only ~38 days left before the deadline as of the knowledge cutoff (May 23), and the new CEO typically needs some time to settle in and finalize the S-1 narrative, the timeline is quite tight. While they could still confidentially file based on Q1 financials by June, the cautious "someday" language suggests they are not aggressively accelerating a Q2 announcement. Therefore, I estimate a 25% probability that the specific announcement or S-1 filing happens before the tight July 1 cutoff, assuming they are more likely targeting a late summer or fall 2025 timeline.
